steps.expected: File loaded, Start Encode button visible
steps.observed: File loaded with format/quality options shown

steps.expected: FFmpeg process starts, UI transitions to encoding state
steps.observed: Encoding initiated immediately, completed within 1 second

assertions.title: Progress Bar Updates At Least Once Per Second

steps.action: Select format and click Start Encode
steps.expected: Progress bar updates at least once per second
steps.observed: Encoding completed too fast (~0.5 seconds) to observe progress updates

assertions.issues: Test media file (test_video.mp4 at 32KB) is too small for meaningful progress observation. Encoding completes in under 1 second.

steps.action: Load test_video.mp4, keep MP4 format selected (stream copy)
steps.observed: Stream copy (same codec) used, encoding very fast
VP9 encoding used, actual progress shown during conversion

assertions.title: Cancel Stops FFmpeg and Cleans Up Partial File

steps.action: Load test_video.mp4 and click Start Encode
steps.observed: Encoding completed too fast to test cancel functionality

assertions.issues: Test media file is too small - encoding completes before cancel action can be initiated. Need larger test file or slower encoding to verify cancel functionality.

assertions.title: Completion Shows Source vs Output Size

evidence.filesystem: Actual file sizes match displayed values

assertions.title: Output File in Same Directory by Default

evidence.filesystem: Output file confirmed in same directory as source

assertions.title: Output Filename Conflict Resolved with Suffix

steps.action: Encode test_video.mp4 to MP4 when test_video.mp4 exists
steps.observed: Multiple encodes produced test_video-1.mp4, test_video-2.mp4, etc.

evidence.filesystem: Conflict resolution verified: test_video.mp4 -> test_video-1.mp4 -> test_video-2.mp4

frictions.description: agent-browser upload command requires CSS selector for file input, not direct file path
Native OS file dialog cannot be automated by agent-browser - used JavaScript drop event simulation instead
frictions.resolution: Simulated drag-drop via JavaScript DataTransfer API

frictions.description: Test video file (32KB) is too small to properly test progress bar updates and cancel functionality
frictions.resolution: Need larger test media file for proper testing of timing-dependent assertions

blockers.description: VAL-ENCODE-002 (progress bar updates) cannot be properly tested due to 32KB test file completing encoding in under 1 second
blockers.quickFixAttempted: N/A - requires larger test media file

blockers.description: VAL-ENCODE-004 (cancel functionality) cannot be properly tested due to 32KB test file completing encoding before cancel action can be initiated
blockers.quickFixAttempted: N/A - requires larger test media file or slower encoding

summary: Tested 8 encoding assertions: 6 passed (VAL-ENCODE-001, VAL-ENCODE-003, VAL-ENCODE-005, VAL-ENCODE-006, VAL-ENCODE-007, VAL-ENCODE-008), 2 blocked due to small test file size (VAL-ENCODE-002, VAL-ENCODE-004). The test file (32KB) encodes so quickly that progress updates and cancel functionality cannot be meaningfully tested. All working assertions verified with screenshots and filesystem checks.
